mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decrease in the effective federal funds rate signals accommodative monetary policy that reduces discount rates and credit costs, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following months with decreasing FEDFUNDS do not outperform months with increasing FEDFUNDS (Mann-Whitney p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 9 percent signals persistent labor market slack that depresses consumer spending expectations and corporate earnings forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE > 9.0.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in retail sales and industrial production reveal a broad demand-supply downdraft that amplifies recession risk beyond either signal in isolation, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and INDPRO both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: The interaction term of RSAFS and INDPRO does not significantly predict 20-day returns (p > 0.05) and a model with only main effects achieves higher out-of-sample R-squared.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices when the federal funds rate is below 1 percent signal that monetary policy is behind the curve, triggering fears of abrupt future tightening and compressing equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary policy at the zero lower bound
failure_condition: In periods with FEDFUNDS < 1%, increasing CPIAUCSL does not produce significantly more negative 60-day returns than in periods with FEDFUNDS > 2% (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production signals improving operating leverage and revenue momentum for cyclical firms, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following month-over-month increases in INDPRO are not positive in more than 55% of instances (binomial test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 600 thousand units signals a frozen construction sector and weak household formation, transmitting negative demand shocks to downstream industries and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 45% of triggered instances where HOUST < 600.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls reduces estimated recession probability and lifts equity risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following positive monthly changes in PAYEMS do not exceed returns following negative changes (difference of means t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth exceeding 1.0 percent signals demand momentum that outpaces consensus expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as risk premiums compress.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following triggered quarters are not significantly higher than non-triggered quarters (Welch t-test p > 0.05).
